DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT TO MENTAL EXAMINATION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL APPEAR FOR MENTAL EXAMINATION BY DR. BERNARD S. RAPPAPORT, M.D. AT HIS MEDICAL OFFICES ON APRIL 15, 2008 AT 9 A.M. THE MENTAL EXAMINATION SHALL CONSIST OF THE TAKING OF PLAINTIFF'S PAST MEDICAL AND PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY; A CLINICAL INTERVIEW AND COMPREHENSIVE ORAL PSYCHIATRIC AND MENTAL STATUS EXAMINATIONS; AND THE ADMINISTRATION OF THE MINNESOTA MULTIPHASIC PERSONALITY INVENTORY 2 (MMPI-2), THE MILLON CLINICAL MULTIAXIAL INVENTORY III (MCMI-III), AND THE ROTTER INCOMPLETE SENTENCES BLANK. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF GUADALUPE PANKRATZ'S MOTION FOR ORDER FOR SERVICE OF PETITION, SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT FOR CIVIL CONSPIRACY AGAINST ATTORNEY. PLAINTIFF MAY SERVE HER PETITION ON DEFENDANTS, WHO MAY SUBMIT THEIR OPPOSING PAPERS IN CONFORMITY WITH CIVIL CODE 1714.10(A) AND THE COURT WILL HEAR THE MATTER PURSUANT TO A SCHEDULE TO BE INCLUDED IN A FORM OF ORDER TO BE SBUMITTED BY PLAINTIFF AND CONSISTENT WITH THE TIME FRAME FOR HEARING A NOTICED MOTION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF GUADALUPE PANKRATS'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT ELANI BENETATOS IN ENGLISH; PROTECTIVE ORDER MAINTAINING CHRONOLOGICAL ORDER OF DEPOSITIONS; FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS; AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE MOTION WAS BROUGHT WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,200, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-23-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: ATTY. JESSE C. RALPH, TEL.776-1826, FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JODY STRUCK AND ATTY. AARON F. MACLEITCH OF HAAPALA THOMPSON & ABERN LLP, TEL.(510)763-2324, FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY ELENI BENETATOS

RULING: A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ELENI BENETATOS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS SUFFICIENT TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED RETALIATORY MOTIVE GIVEN THE STATUTORY PRESUMPTION AND DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON HIS OWN TESTIMONY AS TO HER STATE OF MIND. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/AL

Ntc Of Mtn An Dmtn For Order Compelling Deposition Of Deftelani Benetatos In English; Protective Order Maintaining Chronological Order Of Deposition; Etc And Req For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Apr-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
Jesse C. Ralph, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and moving party: Guadalupe Pankratz
George Benetatos, Esq., appeared for defendant and opposing party: Eleni Benetatos
Aaron MacLeitch, Esq., appeared for defendant and opposing party: Eleni Benetatos
Thomas A. Libatique, CSR # 1550

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Guadalupe Pankrats's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Elani Benetatos In English; Protective Order Maintaining Chronological Order Of Depositions; For Evidentiary Sanctions; And For Monetary Sanctions is denied. The court finds that the motion was brought without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff and her counsel in the amount of $1,200, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice And Motion For Order Compelling Pltf To Submit For Mental Examination; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Apr-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff To Submit To Mental Examination is granted. Plaintiff shall appear for mental examination by Dr. Bernard S. Rappaport, M.D. at his medical offices on April 15, 2008 at 9 a.m.
The mental examination shall consist of the taking of Plaintiff's past medical and psychiatric history; a clinical interview and comprehensive oral psychiatric and mental status examinations; and the administration of the Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory 2 (MMPI- 2), the Millon Clinical Multiaxial Inventory III (MCMI- III), and the Rotter Incomplete Sentences Blank. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED
